steps.title: Recognize squares
steps.content:
The expression inside the parentheses is a difference of squares. The term $144y^2$ can be rewritten as $(12y)^2$, so the expression has the form $a^2-b^2$:
$$
x^2-144y^2=x^2-(12y)^2
$$

steps.title: Factor the difference
steps.content:
Use the identity $a^2-b^2=(a-b)(a+b)$. Here, $a=x$ and $b=12y$, so the two factors must use $x-12y$ and $x+12y$:
$$
x^2-144y^2=(x-12y)(x+12y)
$$

steps.title: Keep the outside factor
steps.content: The original expression has an outside factor of $9$, so keep that factor attached to the factored expression: $$ 9(x-12y)(x+12y) $$ This matches the choice with the same outside factor and both conjugate factors. The answer is D.
